name: Hanzo di Teufel
description: Baron Hanzo di Teufel -- the campaign's master villain: a charismatic borderland noble and fire-wielding arcane sorcerer/demonologist who commands the Dragon Knights, runs possession rituals, and secretly seeks to become a Dragon King.
narrative: Baron Hanzo di Teufel is a rising political power in the borderlands and the true 'power behind the throne' -- urbane, magnetic and utterly ruthless (Deceit 93, Influence 92, Willpower 94). A master arcane sorcerer steeped in fire magic and demonology, he binds spirits into living flesh and is engineering a possession scheme to win the Lake Lady's Tourney; his ultimate aim is dragon transformation -- to rise as a Dragon King. He commands the Dragon Knights ('Dragonshits' to the locals) and arrives on the steam-wreathed DragonBarge. He is the father, by an elven mage, of the twins Randall and Conall, and of his cruel heir Santo.

extras.class_note: Arcane sorcerer / fire demonologist; high-rank master caster (effectively Rank 3-4 Mage given his 18-spell breadth incl. Lightning Bolt, Wall of Fire, Charm Monster, Hold Person).
extras.role: Main villain -- Baron of the borderlands, 'power behind the throne'; fire magic, demonology, spirit-binding possession rituals; ultimate aim: become a Dragon King.
extras.magic_theme: Charm/domination (Charm Being/Monster, Suggestion, Hypnotism, Sleep, Hold Person, Friendship, Confusion, Fumble) + fire & force (Wall of Fire, Lightning Bolt, Magic Missile) + utility (Fly, Shield, Mage Lock, Locate Object, Detect/Read Magic).
extras.commands: The Dragon Knights ('Dragonshits'); arrives on the DragonBarge
extras.family: Father (by an elven mage) of the twins Randall & Conall, and of his heir Santo di Teufel
extras.lore_items: Demonologist's soul-knife bearing the symbol of Asmodeus (binds spirits to victims' flesh) -- per setting lore; not on the Roll20 weapon list
extras.sheet_cleanup_note: On the Roll20 sheet Arcane Casting & Arcane Knowledge were left at 0 (unusable for the villain sorcerer) -- supplied working values (Casting 88, Knowledge 80); GM adjust as desired. Excluded as artifacts: a stray 'Animism' tradition (rank 1) and the 0-value 'Trance'/'false' professional skills. No weapons were entered on the sheet.
extras.source: Roll20 Classic Fantasy character sheet (And then the Dragons Came campaign)
